# KITEBI KILEMIĀT·I TAKES ITS DRESS — the styled phrasebook + the plural junction law — (R-060 · 2026-07-21)

**2026-07-21** · the styling pass (HANDOFF §4.4, aesthetics last) · Design System: `../docs/design-system/`

---

## The styled book

**`deliverables/kitebi-kilemiat.html`** — generated by `tools/phrasebook-html.js`, never hand-edited. The phrasebook is a true sibling of the Companion Book:

- **Tokens verbatim** from the Design System (deep night `#0b0b12`, gold leaf, copper, lapis, teal readouts; Crimson Pro / EB Garamond / IBM Plex Mono / Amiri) + **Noto Sans Coptic** as a documented phrasebook addition for the everyday script.
- The `.aq-field` concentric ripple, uppercase-mono eyebrows at 0.18em, 1px hairline outlines (never shadows), 12px panels, 20px chips — the system's law throughout.
- **Cover**: the copper Hedronite mark · the title in all four scripts · the stats line in teal mono ("116 PHRASES · 12 CHAPTERS · 4 SCRIPTS · R-049–R-059 · NOTHING HAND-TYPED" — precision is the reassurance) · the Ancestors' charge.
- **Entries**: continuous 001–116 mono numbering; display form in Crimson Pro; English in Garamond italic; the three lived scripts in a grid — community (teal mono), Coptic (copper-light), Ajami (Amiri, lapis, RTL). Register chips: sacred = gold fill, plain = teal outline, everyday = hairline.
- **Chapters** carry Amraqiyyah names: Silemiāt · Fiḍeli · Sanupa · Homipon · Ib · ʿIbedi · Na·šaʿab · Na·yak ma na·pet · Na·hina · Sabeʾow · Ḥosibonāt · Tajerow.
- Print CSS: chapter page-breaks, entries unbroken — the PDF booklet is one Cmd-P away.
- A quiet gift discovered in render: mid-word hiatus ā takes the madda carrier — whose canonical precedent is the word **قرآن** itself.

## R-060 · The plural vowel-junction law (styling forced it)

Naming the courtesy chapter surfaced a three-way divergence on vowel-final plurals: the sealed corpus wrote **kilemiāt / jiwebiāt** (i kept), R-034's text wrote **pokniyāt** (glide written) beside **iškāt** (a merged), the corpus wrote **seba·āt** (hiatus), and the engine produced **kilemyāt** (i replaced). One word could not be spelled four ways — least of all the phrasebook's own name.

Ruled under R-058, on the minimal-amendment principle with the sealed title as governing evidence:

- **u + āt → wāt** — thrice ratified (*saʾwāt, weldwāt, fekwāt*), stands.
- **a + āt → āt** — a+ā merges (*iškāt*). Erratum: corpus *seba·āt* → **sebāt** (one line).
- **i + āt → iāt, written plain** — the glide is spoken /ijāt/ but unwritten, keeping the stem visible (*kilemiāt, jiwebiāt, silemiāt*). Erratum: spec §3.4 *pokniyāt* → **pokniāt** (one word).

Engine corrected; spec §5.2 carries the law; **five new goldens** — including `pluralize('kilemi') → kilemiāt`: the book's name now proves itself on every test run. Tests **87/87**.

*Ḏakira, saša, ma la nasiya.*
